Turn 35 ‚ÄĒ the first A2 -secondary tower closes for all root degrees The standing question is still whether the profinite one-relator quotients őďS and őďH are isomorphic over the fixed quotient G, i.e. whether there is a continuous ő¶:őďS‚ÜíőďH with ŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.

The new conclusion is:Bd‚Ü†Hd(2)for¬†every¬†d‚Č•9.Together with Turns 31, 33, and 34, this clears the entire first A2=R/(4,t) secondary coefficient layer in every root degree d‚Č•4. It still does not prove finite coefficient nonemptiness over all Artinian quotients of R‚ČÉZ2 [[t]], because the iterated m2=(4,2t,t2) coefficient layer remains.Recall the local coefficient setup. On the dangerous Ef-piece,

R:=Efőõ‚ČÉZ2 [[t]],t=s‚ąí1,and after the H1 -change the row is(s2+1,¬†s‚ąí1)=(2+2t+t2,¬†t).

The first t-layer R/(2,t2) was shown contractible: the row becomes (0,t), and a second-coordinate correction kills any leading t-tail without producing a same-layer restricted-square obstruction.

For the first 2-layer A2=R/(4,t), Turn 30 split off the lower 2-ghost square-neutrally. The true obstruction group is not Ld[2]itself butHd(2):=Ld[2]/imőłd0,whereőłd0:ker(Ld‚äēLd(u,v)‚Ü¶[A,u]+[B,v]Ld+1)‚ü∂Ld[2],őłd0 (u,v)=u[2]+v[2].This came from the square-neutral section U‚Ü¶(U,0,U), which kills every lower 2-ghost U without contributing a restricted-square shadow.

The resulting secondary quotient Hd(2)is the only possible first-A2 obstruction.

Turns 31‚Äď34 already cleared the base block:H4(2),H5(2),H6(2),H7(2),H8(2)are all killed. In particular, Turn 34 showed that the exceptional residual d=8 codimension-one miss disappears after quotienting by imőł80, because őł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]is not in the residual high hyperplane.

The first A2 -secondary tower was therefore clear through d=8, but Turn 34 left d‚Č•9 as the next uniform target.

Now define a coefficient-safe root space Cd(2)‚äÜLd: a primitive W‚ąąLd lies in Cd(2)if it can be introduced as a temporary degree-d root direction while all lower residual and first-A2 coefficient defects remain continuably zero. The claim isCd(2)=Ld (d‚Č•9).This follows by the same recursion as the residual induction, but with the base block d=4,‚Ä¶,8 supplied by the coefficient calculations.

If d is odd, then the restricted-Lie recursion givesLd=im‚ąād‚ąí1,‚ąād‚ąí1 (U,V)=[A,U]+[B,V].

So any W‚ąąLd is introduced by an ordinary degree-(d‚ąí1) correction in the Y1/q coordinates. Its forced lower square effects occur in already smaller root degrees, hence are killed by the coefficient-safe base/induction hypothesis. Thus W‚ąąCd(2).

If d is even, thenLd/im‚ąād‚ąí1‚ČÖLd/2[2].

Modulo an ordinary boundary, every W‚ąąLd has the form Z[2]for some Z‚ąąLd/2. Since d/2<d, the induction hypothesis gives Z‚ąąCd/2(2). Its square transgression supplies Z[2], and the remaining boundary part is introduced by an ordinary degree-(d‚ąí1) correction. Therefore W‚ąąCd(2).

This provesCd(2)=Ld (d‚Č•9).

Now take any class inHd(2)=Ld[2]/imőłd0.

It is represented by W[2]for some W‚ąąLd. Since W‚ąąCd(2), choose an actual restricted representative of the corresponding temporary root deformation and lift it to the A2=R/(4,t) coefficient problem. Its residual part is continuably zero below degree 2d, while its coefficient-2 Bockstein shadow at degree 2d is precisely W[2], modulo the already-quotiented imőłd0. Therefore the continuation-Bockstein map satisfiesBd (that¬†deformation)=[W[2]]‚ąąHd(2).

Since W was arbitrary,Bd‚Ü†Hd(2)(d‚Č•9).

So the first A2 -secondary layer is fully clear:Bd‚Ü†Hd(2)(d‚Č•4).The conceptual gain is that d=8 was the only apparent exceptional root because the residual high torsor had a codimension-one miss there. After the secondary quotient imőł80, even that exception vanished. For d‚Č•9, the residual root induction Cd=Ld already supplies enough coefficient-safe root directions, once the lower d<9 coefficient base block is included. The residual induction itself had already shown Cm=Lm for every m‚Č•5, and that every even square quotient is hit by W‚Ü¶W[2].This still does not finish the over-G isomorphism proof. What is now closed is the whole first coefficient layer:m/m2=(2,t)/(4,2t,t2).The first t-direction is contractible, and the first 2-direction has no surviving secondary obstruction in any root degree. The next genuine coefficient problem is not d=9 or d=10; it is the coefficient-grade two ghost layerm2/m3=‚ü®4,¬†2t,¬†t2‚ü©.That layer should be treated as one coupled ghost complex, not as three unrelated cases. In the associated graded ringgrm

R‚ČÖF2 [őĪ,ő≤],őĪ=in(2),ő≤=in(t),the leading row is (őĪ,ő≤). Thus the degree-two coefficient ghosts are governed by the Koszul relations among őĪ2,őĪő≤,ő≤2, together with the actual second-order term 2t+t2 in s2+1=2+2t+t2. This is the next structural object.

ESTABLISHED: The general first-A2 statement is proved:Bd‚Ü†Hd(2)for¬†every¬†d‚Č•9.

Together with the prior base block d=4,‚Ä¶,8, all first A2=R/(4,t) secondary coefficient obstructions vanish. The first t=s‚ąí1 layer R/(2,t2) is already contractible, so the full first coefficient layer m/m2 is clear.

GAP: Finite coefficient nonemptiness over every Artinian quotient of R‚ČÉZ2 [[t]] is still not proved. The remaining obstruction problem begins at coefficient grade two, where the coupled 4,2t,t2 ghosts and the second-order term 2t+t2 in s2+1 must be organized into a uniform m-adic ghost induction.

NEXT: Build the coefficient-grade-two ghost complex overgrm2

R=‚ü®őĪ2,őĪő≤,ő≤2‚ü©,including the Koszul relation for the leading row (őĪ,ő≤), the second-order row contribution 2t+t2, and the Bockstein squares of first-layer corrections. Test whether this grade-two complex reduces to the already-cleared first-layer maps Bd‚Ü†Hd(2).[TURN35-FIRST-A2-ALL-D-CLEARED]
